MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Jazmyne Arnett of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON & SEAL LLP (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant Foley-PMI, Inc.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date is March 27, 2023 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is April 7, 2023. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.

The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is March 23, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation.

Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.

Fact discovery cut-off date is March 10, 2023. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 24, 2023. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than January 12, 2023. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents Pursuant To Subpoena
Off Calendar Aug-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nicolas P. Martin of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-9262), appearing for defendant / moving party Honeywell International Inc.
Ryan C. Kujawski of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON & SEAL LLP (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant / joinder Brand Insulations, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Ernestine Brown as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Paul Brown, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO SUBPOENA

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Honeywell International Inc.'s motion to compel production of documents is denied. Notice of the subpoena is defective pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 1985.3, as the Notice to Consumer and the Subpoena do not include completed and signed proofs of service. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 1989, the Court lacks jurisdiction to compel Dr. Timothy Gallagher to appear for deposition because he is a resident of Oregon, and thus lacks jurisdiction to compel Dr. Gallagher to produce the medical records of Monte Manwill at the deposition. Even if the Court reached the merits of this motion, it would not be inclined to grant it. Balancing the privacy interest of Mr. Manwill in not disclosing his medical records as a witness in this action with the countervailing public interest in disclosure, Mr. Manwill's privacy interest outweighs Defendant's interest in contesting his credibility.
Defendant Brand Insulations, Inc.'s joinder to defendant Honeywell International Inc.'s motion to compel the production of documents. No argument made. Tentative ruling adopted: Joinder's motion is denied pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 2.7(B) and 2.11(T) for failure to lodge with the Court a courtesy copy of defendant's moving papers.
One order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Diana L. Gonzalez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #7935 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-27-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nicolas P. Martin of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant / moving party Honeywell International Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Ernestine Brown as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Paul Brown, deceased, et al

RULING:
DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Honeywell International Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Paul Brown was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's Separate Statement on this topic fails to raise the sufficiency of plaintiffs' special discovery responses and it violates CRC 3.1350(d)(3). (UMF 17-22.) Regardless, plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories and the deposition testimony of Monte Manwill preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Additionally, the limited questioning of Mr. Manwill did not demonstrate that he does not have knowledge supporting plaintiffs' claims against defendant. Defendant's arguments related to substantial factor causation fail because such arguments are only based on whether plaintiffs have evidence to demonstrate a threshold exposure. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied, as it is solely based on the same grounds as summary judgment. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Gary Miles are overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
